items.label: Scheffler Defends:
items.body: Scottie Scheffler returns to defend his CJ Cup Byron Nelson title at TPC Craig Ranch, where he headlines the week as the clear focal point of the field.

items.label: Texas Homecoming:
items.body: The event stays in North Texas at TPC Craig Ranch in McKinney, keeping the tournament in a familiar birdie-friendly setting with strong local interest.

items.label: Post-Major Reset:
items.body: The tournament lands right after the PGA Championship stretch, so it serves as a bit of a reset week for players who want to keep momentum going or bounce back fast.

items.label: Field Strength:
items.body: The latest field is a full 144-player event, with Scheffler joined by names like Jordan Spieth, Si Woo Kim, Aaron Rai, Wyndham Clark, and Cam Davis.

items.label: Low-Score Setup:
items.body: TPC Craig Ranch is expected to play as a scorer’s course again, which usually means plenty of birdies, a tight leaderboard, and one hot putter making a huge difference.

items.label: Scottie Scheffler (+220 to +300):
items.body: The defending champion and runaway betting favorite in the latest market, with elite all-around form and obvious comfort on this course.

items.label: Jordan Spieth (+1400 to +1800):
items.body: A hometown draw with real upside on a course that can reward creativity, wedge play, and a hot week on the greens.

items.label: Wyndham Clark (+1800 to +2200):
items.body: A high-ceiling power player who can pile up birdies quickly if the driver stays under control.

items.label: Aaron Rai (+2200 to +2800):
items.body: A steady, accurate ball striker who fits a course where precision can keep you in the hunt all week.

items.label: Si Woo Kim (+2500 to +3000):
items.body: A volatile but dangerous contender whose upside rises on a low-scoring setup like this one.

items.label: Tom Kim (+2500 to +3500):
items.body: A young star with enough firepower and shot-making to get hot fast on a course that often turns into a birdie contest.

items.label: Davis Riley (+3000 to +4000):
items.body: A solid mid-tier option whose consistency and iron play can play well in a field where someone needs to stay aggressive.

items.label: Cam Davis (+3500 to +4500):
items.body: A big hitter who can make noise if he starts taking advantage of the par-5s and shorter scoring holes.

items.label: Eric Cole (+3500 to +4500):
items.body: A strong all-around player with enough touch and short-game skill to hang around if the leaderboard tightens.

items.label: Venue:
items.body: TPC Craig Ranch in McKinney, Texas.

items.label: Defending Champion:
items.body: Scottie Scheffler won the event last year and returns to try to repeat.

items.label: Event Identity:
items.body: The CJ Cup Byron Nelson has become one of the more scoreable stops on the schedule, with winning scores often getting very low.

items.label: Past Winners:
items.body: Recent champions include Taylor Pendrith, K.H. Lee, Aaron Wise, and Billy Horschel.

items.label: Course:
items.body: TPC Craig Ranch, McKinney, Texas.

items.label: Dates:
items.body: May 21 through May 24, 2026.

items.label: Purse:
items.body: $10.3 million.

items.label: Format:
items.body: 144-player, 72-hole stroke play event.

items.label: Yardage:
items.body: 7,468 yards, par 72.

items.label: Coverage:
items.body: Standard PGA Tour broadcast and streaming coverage is expected for the week.

items.label: Event Window:
items.body: Competition runs May 21 through May 24, 2026.
